title: Castle in the Air - Contents (Fragment 1)
text:
Castle in the Air

I N WHICH A BDULLAH BUYS A CARPET

I N WHICH A BDULLAH IS MISTAKEN FOR A YOUNG LADY

I N WHICH F LOWER-IN-THE -N IGHT DISCOVERS SEVERAL IMPORTANT FACTS

Chapter Four

W HICH CONCERNS MARRIAGE AND PROPHECY

W HICH TELLS HOW F LOWER-IN-THE -N IGHT ’ S FATHER WISHED TO RAISE A BDULLAH ABOVE ALL OTHERS IN THE LAND

Chapter Six

W HICH SHOWS HOW A BDULLAH WENT FROM THE FRYING PAN INTO THE FIRE

Chapter Seven

W HICH INTRODUCES THE GENIE

Chapter Eight

I N WHICH A BDULLAH’S DREAMS CONTINUE TO COME TRUE

Chapter Nine

I N WHICH A BDULLAH ENCOUNTERS AN OLD SOLDIER

Chapter Ten

W HICH TELLS OF VIOLENCE AND BLOODSHED

Chapter Eleven

I N WHICH A WILD ANIMAL CAUSES A BDULLAH TO WASTE A WISH

Chapter Twelve

I N WHICH THE LAW CATCHES UP WITH A BDULLAH AND THE SOLDIER

Chapter Thirteen

I N WHICH A BDULLAH CHALLENGES F ATE

Chapter Fourteen

W HICH TELLS HOW THE MAGIC CARPET REAPPEARED

Chapter Fifteen

I N WHICH THE TRAVELERS ARRIVE AT K INGSBURY

Chapter Sixteen

I N WHICH STRANGE THINGS BEFALL M IDNIGHT AND W HIPPERSNAPPER

Chapter Seventeen

I N WHICH A BDULLAH AT LAST REACHES THE CASTLE IN THE AIR

Chapter Eighteen

W HICH IS RATHER FULL OF PRINCESSES

Chapter Nineteen

I N WHICH A SOLDIER, A COOK, AND A CARPET SELLER ALL STATE THEIR PRICE

Chapter Twenty

I N WHICH A DJINN’S LIFE IS FOUND AND THEN HIDDEN

Chapter Twenty-One

I N WHICH THE CASTLE COMES DOWN TO EARTH

vocabulary.definition: (Especially of something bad) happen to someone.
vocabulary.example: Strange things befall Midnight and Whippersnapper.

vocabulary.definition: The killing or wounding of people, usually in a violent way.
vocabulary.example: Which tells of violence and bloodshed.

vocabulary.definition: A floor covering made from thick woven fabric.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah buys a carpet.

vocabulary.definition: A large building or group of buildings fortified against attack.
vocabulary.example: Castle in the Air.

vocabulary.definition: To reach someone or something ahead by moving faster.
vocabulary.example: The law catches up with Abdullah and the soldier.

vocabulary.definition: Tests the ability, quality, or strength of something; disputes or questions.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah challenges fate.

vocabulary.definition: Relates to or is about something.
vocabulary.example: Which concerns marriage and prophecy.

vocabulary.definition: Finds something previously unknown or unseen.
vocabulary.example: Flower-in-the-Night discovers several important facts.

vocabulary.definition: A supernatural spirit in Arabian and Muslim mythology, often granting wishes.
vocabulary.example: In which a djinn’s life is found and then hidden.

vocabulary.definition: Meets unexpectedly or faces.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah encounters an old soldier.

vocabulary.definition: A male parent.
vocabulary.example: Flower-in-the-Night’s father wished to raise Abdullah above all others.

translations.ru: сковорода, в которой готовят (фразеологизм: из огня да в полымя)

vocabulary.definition: A cooking pan used for frying food; used metaphorically as in the phrase 'from the frying pan into the fire'.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah went from the frying pan into the fire.

vocabulary.definition: Presents someone or something for the first time.
vocabulary.example: Which introduces the genie.

vocabulary.definition: Incorrectly identified or understood; confused with something else.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah is mistaken for a young lady.

vocabulary.definition: The amount of money expected or required for something.
vocabulary.example: A soldier, a cook, and a carpet seller all state their price.

vocabulary.definition: A prediction of what will happen in the future.
vocabulary.example: Which concerns marriage and prophecy.

vocabulary.definition: Lifts or elevates; brings up or nurtures.
vocabulary.example: Father wished to raise Abdullah above all others in the land.

vocabulary.definition: Appeared again after being absent.
vocabulary.example: Which tells how the magic carpet reappeared.

vocabulary.definition: A person who serves in an army.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah encounters an old soldier.

vocabulary.definition: Unusual or surprising; difficult to understand.
vocabulary.example: Strange things befall Midnight and Whippersnapper.

vocabulary.definition: People who are travelling or who often travel.
vocabulary.example: The travelers arrive at Kingsbury.

vocabulary.definition: Physical force intended to hurt or damage.
vocabulary.example: Which tells of violence and bloodshed.

vocabulary.definition: Use or expend carelessly, extravagantly, or to no purpose.
vocabulary.example: A wild animal causes Abdullah to waste a wish.

vocabulary.definition: Living or growing in the natural environment; not domesticated or controlled.
vocabulary.example: A wild animal causes Abdullah to waste a wish.
